7	
8	// StopCondition is the typed work-drain terminal decision vocabulary. Keep
9	// these values stable; evidence and operator surfaces derive from them.
10	type StopCondition string
11	
12	const (
13		StopConditionDrained    StopCondition = "Drained"
14		StopConditionOnce       StopCondition = "Once"
15		StopConditionSignal     StopCondition = "Signal"
16		StopConditionBudget     StopCondition = "Budget"
17		StopConditionNoProgress StopCondition = "NoProgress"
18		StopConditionBlocked    StopCondition = "Blocked"
19	)
20	
21	// StopInput describes one possible terminal drain decision point.
22	type StopInput struct {
23		ContextErr   error
24		NoReadyWork  bool
25		Once         bool
26		PollInterval time.Duration
27		Budget       bool
28		NoProgress   bool
29		Blocked      bool
30	}
31	
32	// StopDecision is the classified terminal decision plus the legacy-compatible
33	// loop.end exit_reason string.
34	type StopDecision struct {
35		Condition  StopCondition
36		ExitReason string
37	}
38	
39	// ClassifyStop maps scattered drain-loop terminal checks to a single typed
40	// vocabulary while preserving existing exit_reason strings.
41	func ClassifyStop(in StopInput) (StopDecision, bool) {
42		switch {
43		case in.ContextErr != nil:
44			return StopDecision{
45				Condition:  StopConditionSignal,
46				ExitReason: signalExitReason(in.ContextErr),
47			}, true
48		case in.Budget:
49			return StopDecision{Condition: StopConditionBudget, ExitReason: "budget"}, true
50		case in.NoProgress:
51			return StopDecision{Condition: StopConditionNoProgress, ExitReason: "no_progress"}, true
52		case in.Blocked:
53			return StopDecision{Condition: StopConditionBlocked, ExitReason: "blocked"}, true
54		case in.Once:
55			return StopDecision{Condition: StopConditionOnce, ExitReason: "once_complete"}, true
56		case in.NoReadyWork && in.PollInterval <= 0:
57			return StopDecision{Condition: StopConditionDrained, ExitReason: "explicit_poll_zero"}, true
58		default:
59			return StopDecision{}, false
60		}
61	}
62	
63	func signalExitReason(err error) string {
64		switch err {
65		case context.Canceled:
66			return "sigint"
67		case context.DeadlineExceeded:
68			return "sigterm"
69		default:
70			return "context_cancelled"
71		}
72	}

1	package escalation
2	
3	import (
4		"fmt"
5		"strings"
6		"sync"
7	)
8	
9	// InfrastructureFailurePatterns are detail substrings that indicate a
10	// transient infrastructure-level failure (provider 502, network unreachable,
11	// command not found, auth/quota exhausted) rather than a model-capability
12	// failure (test failed, build failed, no changes after attempt).
13	//
14	// Infrastructure failures should not consume escalation budget — the bead
15	// should be deferred with a retry-after, not retried at the next tier with
16	// a more expensive model. The model wasn't given a fair chance.
17	//
18	// Patterns are matched case-insensitively as substrings of the failure
19	// detail string. Any new pattern added here is automatically picked up by
20	// IsInfrastructureFailure callers.
21	var InfrastructureFailurePatterns = []string{
22		// HTTP-level provider unavailability
23		"502", "503", "504",
24		"bad gateway",
25		"service unavailable",
26		"gateway timeout",
27		// Network-level unreachability
28		"connection refused",
29		"no such host",
30		"no route to host",
31		"network is unreachable",
32		"i/o timeout",
33		"context deadline exceeded",
34		// Auth/quota exhaustion (operator-fixable, not a model fault)
35		"401", "429",
36		"unauthorized",
37		"rate limit",
38		"ratelimit",
39		"quota exceeded",
40		"insufficient quota",
41		"insufficient_quota",
42		// Subprocess harness binary missing or unstartable
43		"command not found",
44		"executable file not found",
45		"no such file or directory",
46	}
47	
48	// IsInfrastructureFailure reports whether the given failure status + detail
49	// indicates a transient infrastructure problem the model could not have
50	// fixed. Only execution_failed can be infrastructure; other escalatable
51	// statuses are semantic outcomes that should proceed through the tier ladder.
52	// Returns false for statuses whose detail does not match any known
53	// infrastructure pattern.
54	//
55	// Used by the execute-loop to decide whether to (a) defer the bead with a
56	// retry-after and try the same tier later, or (b) burn through to the next
57	// tier as the standard escalation policy.
58	func IsInfrastructureFailure(status, detail string) bool {
59		if status != "execution_failed" {
60			return false
61		}
62		if detail == "" {
63			return false
64		}
65		lower := strings.ToLower(detail)
66		for _, p := range InfrastructureFailurePatterns {
67			if strings.Contains(lower, p) {
68				return true
69			}
70		}
71		return false
72	}
73	
74	// DefaultMaxCostUSD is the per-execute-loop dollar cap on accumulated billed
75	// spend. Exceeding the cap halts further bead claiming so an execute-loop
76	// run cannot silently burn through arbitrary openrouter / pay-per-token
77	// credits. Local LLMs and subscription-bundled providers do not count
78	// toward the cap (see CountsTowardCostCap).
79	//
80	// $100 is large enough not to interrupt normal subscription-driven work
81	// (where billed cost stays at $0) and small enough to surface a clear stop
82	// signal before runaway openrouter usage.
83	const DefaultMaxCostUSD = 100.0
84	
85	// CostClassBilled is the HarnessInfo.CostClass value the agent uses for
86	// pay-per-token providers. Free local providers report "free" and
87	// subscription-bundled providers report "subscription".
88	const CostClassBilled = "expensive"
89	
90	// CountsTowardCostCap returns true when an attempt's reported CostUSD
91	// should accumulate against the loop's MaxCostUSD. Callers pass the
92	// HarnessInfo of the harness that ran the attempt; when info is nil the
93	// safe default is to count (treat unknown as billed).
94	func CountsTowardCostCap(isLocal, isSubscription bool, costClass string) bool {
95		if isLocal {
96			return false
97		}
98		if isSubscription {
99			return false
100		}
101		// CostClass "free" or "subscription" never bill; only "expensive" does.
102		if costClass != "" && costClass != CostClassBilled {
103			return false
104		}
105		return true
106	}
107	
108	// HarnessBilledLookup reports whether a harness's reported CostUSD should
109	// be accumulated against a CostCapTracker. Implementations typically
110	// consult a service.ListHarnesses snapshot. A nil lookup is treated as
111	// "count by default" (the safe option for unknown harnesses).
112	type HarnessBilledLookup func(harnessName string) bool
113	
114	// CostCapTracker accumulates billed cost for a single execute-loop run
115	// and reports when accumulated spend has reached the configured cap.
116	// Safe for concurrent use across goroutines (workers may call Add and
117	// Tripped concurrently).
118	//
119	// MaxUSD == 0 disables the cap; Tripped always returns (_, false). The
120	// Lookup callback decides which harnesses contribute to the running
121	// total — local and subscription-bundled providers should be excluded
122	// (see CountsTowardCostCap).
123	type CostCapTracker struct {
124		MaxUSD float64
125		Lookup HarnessBilledLookup
126	
127		mu    sync.Mutex
128		spent float64
129		// cache memoizes Lookup results so we do not rebuild a service for
130		// every reported attempt. Protected by mu.
131		cache map[string]bool
132	}
133	
134	// NewCostCapTracker constructs a tracker with the given dollar cap and
135	// lookup. Pass maxUSD <= 0 to disable the cap. A nil lookup is treated
136	// as "count by default" — every harness contributes.
137	func NewCostCapTracker(maxUSD float64, lookup HarnessBilledLookup) *CostCapTracker {
138		return &CostCapTracker{
139			MaxUSD: maxUSD,
140			Lookup: lookup,
141			cache:  map[string]bool{},
142		}
143	}
144	
145	// counts reports whether harnessName's CostUSD should accumulate. The
146	// result is memoized; callers that want fresh resolution should
147	// construct a new tracker. Lookups for empty harness names default to
148	// true (count) so accidentally-missing harness metadata never silently
149	// bypasses the cap.
150	func (t *CostCapTracker) counts(harnessName string) bool {
151		if harnessName == "" {
152			return true
153		}
154		t.mu.Lock()
155		if v, ok := t.cache[harnessName]; ok {
156			t.mu.Unlock()
157			return v
158		}
159		t.mu.Unlock()
160		result := true
161		if t.Lookup != nil {
162			result = t.Lookup(harnessName)
163		}
164		t.mu.Lock()
165		t.cache[harnessName] = result
166		t.mu.Unlock()
167		return result
168	}
169	
170	// Add accumulates costUSD against the running total when harnessName's
171	// billing class counts toward the cap. Non-positive cost is ignored.
172	func (t *CostCapTracker) Add(harnessName string, costUSD float64) {
173		if costUSD <= 0 {
174			return
175		}
176		if !t.counts(harnessName) {
177			return
178		}
179		t.mu.Lock()
180		t.spent += costUSD
181		t.mu.Unlock()
182	}
183	
184	// Spent returns the current accumulated billed total.
185	func (t *CostCapTracker) Spent() float64 {
186		t.mu.Lock()
187		defer t.mu.Unlock()
188		return t.spent
189	}
190	
191	// Tripped reports whether the accumulated spend has reached MaxUSD. When
192	// MaxUSD <= 0 the cap is disabled and Tripped returns (_, false).
193	// Returns the formatted operator-facing detail string when tripped so
194	// callers can populate a stop-the-loop ExecuteBeadReport.
195	func (t *CostCapTracker) Tripped() (string, bool) {
196		if t.MaxUSD <= 0 {
197			return "", false
198		}
199		t.mu.Lock()
200		spent := t.spent
201		t.mu.Unlock()
202		if spent < t.MaxUSD {
203			return "", false
204		}
205		return fmt.Sprintf("cost cap reached: $%.2f billed >= $%.2f cap; raise the cap or set 0 to disable. Subscription and local providers do not count.", spent, t.MaxUSD), true
206	}

22	// ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime carries the non-serializable plumbing and
23	// per-invocation runtime intent for an execute-bead loop run. Durable knobs
24	// (assignee, retry caps, harness/model, tier bounds, etc.) live on
25	// config.ResolvedConfig and are passed via Run's rcfg argument.
26	type ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime struct {
27		Log             io.Writer
28		CleanupLog      io.Writer
29		CleanupRunner   executionCleanupRunner
30		CleanupInterval time.Duration
31		CleanupTickCh   <-chan time.Time
32		EventSink       io.Writer
33		ProgressCh      chan<- ProgressEvent
34		PreClaimHook    func(ctx context.Context) error
35		// PreClaimIntakeHook runs after routing preflight and before Claim. It
36		// classifies the candidate for actionability/scope; only
37		// actionable_atomic proceeds directly to Claim. Non-atomic outcomes
38		// skip the bead without claim. intake_error fail-opens so infrastructure
39		// failures do not become hidden dispatch failures.
40		PreClaimIntakeHook    PreClaimIntakeHook
41		PreDispatchLintHook   func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (LintResult, error)
42		PostAttemptTriageHook func(ctx context.Context, beadID string, report ExecuteBeadReport) (TriageResult, error)
43		// ReviewCostCap, when non-nil, accumulates reviewer cost on the same
44		// loop budget tracker used by the implementer attempts.
45		ReviewCostCap *escalation.CostCapTracker
46		// RoutePreflight, when non-nil, runs once during Run bootstrap before
47		// the drain loop starts. It is expected to call upstream ResolveRoute
48		// against the loop's resolved (harness, model) and return whatever typed
49		// routing error the upstream surfaced — notably
50		// agent.ErrHarnessModelIncompatible when the harness allow-list rejects
51		// the model. Any non-nil error stops the worker before any bead is
52		// claimed; DDx does NOT duplicate the upstream allow-list.
53		RoutePreflight func(ctx context.Context, harness, model string) error
54		// BudgetStop, when non-nil, is checked before selecting the next bead. It
55		// lets CLI/server callers surface already-tripped budget state as a typed
56		// work-layer StopCondition before any new bead is claimed.
57		BudgetStop   func() (ExecuteBeadReport, bool)
58		Once         bool
59		PollInterval time.Duration
60		NoReview     bool
61		LabelFilter  string
62		SessionID    string
63		WorkerID     string
64		ProjectRoot  string
65		// TargetBeadID, when non-empty, restricts nextCandidate to only return the
66		// named bead from the execution-ready queue. Used by `ddx try <bead-id>`
67		// to dispatch a single specific bead through the same claim → executor →
68		// land path the queue drain uses. When empty, the picker behaves normally.

71		// so the loop re-scans the queue. Used by the operator-prompt approve /

73		// before a freshly-approved bead is claimed. Implementations must send

84	// MaxLoopCooldown is the absolute upper bound the execute-loop will set for
85	// any execute-loop-retry-after value. Year-scale parks effectively mean
86	// "never retry" and that should be a deliberate operator choice via
87	// `ddx bead update --set execute-loop-retry-after=...`, not an automatic
88	// loop decision. Beyond this cap, the loop refuses to lengthen the cooldown
89	// further; an operator extending it manually still works.

93	// paths fail (3-way ort auto-merge + optional focused-resolve agent) and the
94	// bead is parked as land_conflict_unresolvable. Much shorter than
95	// MaxLoopCooldown because land conflicts often resolve quickly as sibling beads
96	// advance the base branch.

100	// the outcome-handling block returns a transient error. It prevents an
101	// immediate re-queue of the affected bead while allowing the loop to continue
102	// to the next candidate. Store errors during outcome handling are non-fatal:
103	// they are logged and emitted as loop-error events but do not terminate the

109	// scheduler state, not bead quality evidence, so it must not become a durable
110	// triage:needs-investigation park.

113	// SubmitWithPreMergeChecks is the canonical land-back step for the execute-bead
114	// loop. It runs the project's .ddx/checks/*.yaml gate against the worker's
115	// (baseRev, resultRev) before forwarding to submit. On Blocked, it preserves
116	// the worker's result under refs/ddx/iterations/, records checks-blocked
117	// events on the bead, and returns the synthesised LandResult{Status:"preserved"}
118	// without ever calling submit. On pass, honoured checks-bypass annotations are
119	// recorded as checks-bypass events and submit runs normally.
120	//
121	// submit is the project's land coordinator entry point (typically
122	// LocalLandCoordinator.Submit or the server-side coordinator submit).
123	//
124	// The implementation lives in checks_premerge.go alongside its supporting
125	// helpers (RunPreMergeChecks, AppendPreMergeChecksEvents,
126	// PreserveAfterPreMergeChecks); this thin wrapper exists so the execute-bead
127	// loop's land-back call site is one obvious helper rather than three nested
128	// ones, and so callers wiring the gate do not need to import the helper layer
129	// directly.
130	func SubmitWithPreMergeChecks(
131		ctx context.Context,
132		projectRoot string,
133		b *bead.Bead,
134		res *ExecuteBeadResult,
135		submit func(LandRequest) (*LandResult, error),
136		eventStore BeadEventAppender,
137		actor, source string,
138		now func() time.Time,
139	) (*LandResult, *PreMergeChecksOutcome, error) {
140		if now == nil {
141			now = time.Now
142		}
143		if submit == nil {
144			return nil, nil, fmt.Errorf("submit-with-pre-merge-checks: submit callback required")
145		}
146		evidenceDir := filepath.Join(projectRoot, res.ExecutionDir)
147		outcome, err := RunPreMergeChecks(ctx, projectRoot, b, res.BaseRev, res.ResultRev, evidenceDir)
148		if err != nil {
149			// Treat checks_bypass / loader errors as a hard preserve: the worker
150			// did its job; the operator misconfigured the gate. Better to park
151			// the result under an iteration ref than to silently land it.
152			ref := PreserveRef(res.BeadID, res.BaseRev)
153			if upErr := (&RealOrchestratorGitOps{}).UpdateRef(projectRoot, ref, res.ResultRev); upErr != nil {
154				return nil, nil, fmt.Errorf("preserving result after checks setup error: %w (original error: %v)", upErr, err)
155			}
156			return &LandResult{
157				Status:      "preserved",
158				PreserveRef: ref,
159				Reason:      "pre-merge checks setup error: " + err.Error(),
160			}, nil, nil
161		}
162		if outcome.Blocked {
163			land, perr := PreserveAfterPreMergeChecks(projectRoot, res, outcome, &RealOrchestratorGitOps{})
164			if perr != nil {
165				return nil, outcome, perr
166			}
167			if eventStore != nil {
168				_ = AppendPreMergeChecksEvents(eventStore, res.BeadID, outcome, actor, source, now().UTC())
169			}
170			return land, outcome, nil
171		}
172		if eventStore != nil && len(outcome.Bypassed) > 0 {
173			_ = AppendPreMergeChecksEvents(eventStore, res.BeadID, outcome, actor, source, now().UTC())
174		}
175		land, sErr := submit(BuildLandRequestFromResult(projectRoot, res))
176		return land, outcome, sErr
177	}

179	// runPostAttemptTriage invokes the optional post-attempt triage hook after
180	// the attempt outcome has been finalized but before any cooldown decision is
181	// applied. Hook failures are fail-open: the report is returned unchanged and
182	// legacy cooldown behavior remains in force.
183	func (w *ExecuteBeadWorker) runPostAttemptTriage(ctx context.Context, candidate bead.Bead, report ExecuteBeadReport, runtime ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime, assignee string, now func() time.Time) ExecuteBeadReport {
184		hook := runtime.PostAttemptTriageHook
185		if hook == nil {
186			return report
187		}
188		triage, err := hook(ctx, candidate.ID, report)
189		if err != nil {
190			if runtime.Log != nil {
191				_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "post-attempt triage error (%s %s): %v (continuing)\n", candidate.ID, report.Status, err)
192			}
193			return report
194		}
195		triage = normalizeTriageResult(triage)
196		hasWarnings := len(triage.DecodeWarnings) > 0 || triage.Malformed
197		classificationRecognized := isRecognizedTriageClassification(triage.Classification)
198		if hasWarnings && runtime.Log != nil {
199			_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "post-attempt triage warning (%s %s): %s (continuing)\n", candidate.ID, report.Status, formatTriageWarnings(triage.DecodeWarnings))
200		}

21	// StopInput describes one possible terminal drain decision point.

23		ContextErr   error
24		NoReadyWork  bool
25		Once         bool
26		PollInterval time.Duration
27		Budget       bool
28		NoProgress   bool

30	}

32	// StopDecision is the classified terminal decision plus the legacy-compatible
33	// loop.end exit_reason string.
34	type StopDecision struct {
35		Condition  StopCondition
36		ExitReason string
37	}
38	
39	// ClassifyStop maps scattered drain-loop terminal checks to a single typed
40	// vocabulary while preserving existing exit_reason strings.
41	func ClassifyStop(in StopInput) (StopDecision, bool) {
42		switch {
43		case in.ContextErr != nil:
44			return StopDecision{
45				Condition:  StopConditionSignal,
46				ExitReason: signalExitReason(in.ContextErr),
47			}, true
48		case in.Budget:
49			return StopDecision{Condition: StopConditionBudget, ExitReason: "budget"}, true
50		case in.NoProgress:
51			return StopDecision{Condition: StopConditionNoProgress, ExitReason: "no_progress"}, true
52		case in.Blocked:
53			return StopDecision{Condition: StopConditionBlocked, ExitReason: "blocked"}, true
54		case in.Once:
55			return StopDecision{Condition: StopConditionOnce, ExitReason: "once_complete"}, true
56		case in.NoReadyWork && in.PollInterval <= 0:
57			return StopDecision{Condition: StopConditionDrained, ExitReason: "explicit_poll_zero"}, true
58		default:
59			return StopDecision{}, false
60		}
61	}

115	// TestStopCondition_BudgetAfterImplementerCostStopsBeforeNextClaim verifies
116	// that after an implementer attempt's cost trips the cap, the BudgetStop
117	// callback fires at the top of the next loop iteration and prevents the next
118	// bead from being claimed. This is the AC3 (ddx-b1cf1f6b) coverage path.
119	func TestStopCondition_BudgetAfterImplementerCostStopsBeforeNextClaim(t *testing.T) {
120		inner, _, _ := newExecuteLoopTestStore(t)
121		store := &claimCountingStore{Store: inner}
122		tracker := escalation.NewCostCapTracker(5.0, func(string) bool { return true })
123	
124		var execCalls int32
125		worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{
126			Store: store,
127			Executor: ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
128				atomic.AddInt32(&execCalls, 1)
129				// Accumulate $6 — trips the $5 cap; mirrors accumulateBilledCost.
130				tracker.Add("openrouter", 6.0)
131				return ExecuteBeadReport{
132					BeadID:    beadID,
133					Status:    ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess,
134					SessionID: "sess-budget-impl",
135					ResultRev: "abc123",
136				}, nil
137			}),
138		}
139	
140		result, exitReason, err := runStopConditionCase(t, worker, context.Background(), ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{
141			BudgetStop: func() (ExecuteBeadReport, bool) {
142				detail, tripped := tracker.Tripped()
143				if !tripped {
144					return ExecuteBeadReport{}, false
145				}
146				return ExecuteBeadReport{
147					Status: ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed,
148					Detail: detail,
149				}, true
150			},
151			PollInterval: 0,
152		})
153		require.NoError(t, err)
154		require.NotNil(t, result)
155		assert.Equal(t, "budget", exitReason)
156		assert.Equal(t, int32(1), atomic.LoadInt32(&execCalls), "first bead must be attempted")
157		assert.Equal(t, int32(1), atomic.LoadInt32(&store.claimCalls), "second bead must not be claimed after budget stop")
158	}
159	
160	// TestStopCondition_BudgetAfterReviewCostPreventsClose verifies that when
161	// reviewer cost trips the budget cap in the APPROVE path, RunPostMergeReview
162	// records the review events but does NOT call CloseWithEvidence and returns
163	// Approved=false. This is the AC2 (ddx-b1cf1f6b) coverage path.
164	func TestStopCondition_BudgetAfterReviewCostPreventsClose(t *testing.T) {
165		store, first, _ := newExecuteLoopTestStore(t)
166		// Pre-charge $4 of $5 cap; reviewer will add $1.5, tripping the cap.
167		tracker := escalation.NewCostCapTracker(5.0, func(string) bool { return true })
168		tracker.Add("openrouter", 4.0)
169	
170		reviewer := makeReviewerWithCost(VerdictApprove, "### Verdict: APPROVE\n\nAll good.", 1.5)
171		out := RunPostMergeReview(context.Background(), PostMergeReviewInput{
172			Bead: *first,
173			Report: ExecuteBeadReport{
174				BeadID:    first.ID,
175				Status:    ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess,
176				SessionID: "sess-budget-review",
177				ResultRev: "deadcafe",
178				CostUSD:   4.0,
179			},
180			Reviewer:      reviewer,
181			Store:         store,
182			ProjectRoot:   t.TempDir(),
183			Rcfg:          config.NewTestConfigForLoop(config.TestLoopConfigOpts{Assignee: "worker"}).Resolve(config.TestLoopOverrides(config.TestLoopConfigOpts{Assignee: "worker"})),
184			Now:           time.Now,
185			Assignee:      "worker",
186			ReviewCostCap: tracker,
187		})
188	
189		require.False(t, out.Approved, "APPROVE whose reviewer cost trips the budget cap must not close the bead")
190		require.NoError(t, out.StoreErr)
191	
192		got, err := store.Get(first.ID)
193		require.NoError(t, err)
194		require.NotEqual(t, bead.StatusClosed, got.Status, "bead must remain open when budget is exceeded by reviewer cost")
195	
196		events, err := store.Events(first.ID)
197		require.NoError(t, err)
198		var foundApprove, foundDeferred bool
199		for _, ev := range events {
200			if ev.Kind == "review" && ev.Summary == "APPROVE" {
201				foundApprove = true
202			}
203			if ev.Kind == "review-cost-deferred" {
204				foundDeferred = true
205			}
206		}
207		assert.True(t, foundApprove, "APPROVE review event must be recorded even when budget exceeded")
208		assert.True(t, foundDeferred, "review-cost-deferred event must be recorded when budget exceeded by reviewer cost")
209	}
210	
211	func runStopConditionCase(t *testing.T, worker *ExecuteBeadWorker, ctx context.Context, runtime ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime) (*ExecuteBeadLoopResult, string, error) {
212		t.Helper()
213	
214		var sink bytes.Buffer
215		runtime.EventSink = &sink
216		runtime.SessionID = "sess-stop-condition"
217		cfgOpts := config.TestLoopConfigOpts{Assignee: "worker"}
218		rcfg := config.NewTestConfigForLoop(cfgOpts).Resolve(config.TestLoopOverrides(cfgOpts))
219		result, err := worker.Run(ctx, rcfg, runtime)
220		exitReason := loopEndExitReason(t, sink.String())
221		return result, exitReason, err
222	}
223	
224	func loopEndExitReason(t *testing.T, raw string) string {
225		t.Helper()
226		for _, line := range strings.Split(strings.TrimSpace(raw), "\n") {
227			if strings.TrimSpace(line) == "" {
228				continue
229			}
230			var entry map[string]any
231			require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(line), &entry))
232			if entry["type"] != "loop.end" {
233				continue
234			}
235			data, ok := entry["data"].(map[string]any)
236			require.True(t, ok, "loop.end data missing")
237			reason, _ := data["exit_reason"].(string)
238			return reason
239		}
240		require.Fail(t, "loop.end event not found", raw)
241		return ""
242	}
